content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array of integers. The array has **even length** and contains an **equal number of positive and negative integers**. Chef wants to rearrange the array so that:


Every consecutive pair of numbers has **opposite signs**.
The **relative order** of positive numbers among themselves and negative numbers among themselves is **preserved**.
The array **starts with a positive number**.
Help Chef rearrange his array according to these rules.

**Function Declaration****Function Name**rearrangeArrayrearrangeArrayrearrangeArray – This function rearranges an array of integers.

**Parameters**

arrarrarr : A reference to a vector of integers of size nnn.



Contains both positive and negative integers.
Has an equal number of positive and negative values.



nnn : An integer representing the size of the array



Always even
Satisfies all constraints


**Return Value**

The function does **not** return anything.




It modifies the vector **in-place**, producing a rearranged array that:



Starts with a positive integer
Alternates sign at every position
Preserves the internal order of all positive and all negative elements


**Constraints**
2≤N≤2⋅1052 \leq N \leq 2 \cdot 10^52≤N≤2⋅105
NNN is even
1≤∣A[i]∣≤1051 \leq |A[i]| \leq 10^51≤∣A[i]∣≤105
The array contains **exactly** N/2N/2N/2 positive and N/2N/2N/2 negative integers

Input Format

The first line contains an integer NNN — the size of the array.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers representing the array elements.


Output Format

For each test case, print the rearranged array (starting with positive and alternating signs) on a new line.
constraints: Exactly N/2 positive and N/2 negative integers
Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 2·10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T — the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an even integer N (2 ≤ N ≤ 2·10^5) — the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers. It is guaranteed that exactly N/2 numbers are positive and N/2 numbers are negative.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the rearranged array. The array must start with a positive integer, signs must alternate, and the relative order of all positive numbers among themselves and all negative numbers among themselves must be preserved. Numbers are printed space‑separated.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Positives in order are [5,2,7,4]; negatives in order are [-1,-3,-2,-6]. Interleaving them starting with a positive reproduces the original array.
Only one positive and one negative; after arranging to start with a positive the result is [10,-7].
Positives [10,20,30] and negatives [-10,-20,-30] are interleaved preserving their internal order.

solution.approach: 1. Scan the array once and collect all positive numbers in a list `pos` preserving their original order. 2. Scan again (or during the same scan) and collect all negative numbers in a list `neg` preserving order. 3. Overwrite the original array by placing `pos[i]` at index 2*i and `neg[i]` at index 2*i+1 for i from 0 to N/2‑1. Because we write to distinct even/odd positions, the operation is O(N) time and O(N) extra space for the two auxiliary lists. The order inside each sign group is never changed, satisfying the stability requirement. 4. Print the modified array. The algorithm works for each test case independently.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the size of the array. The total over all test cases is O(∑N) ≤ O(2·10^5).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) auxiliary space for the two temporary lists (each holds N/2 elements).

code.Python3:
import sys

def rearrange(arr):
    pos=[x for x in arr if x>0]
    neg=[x for x in arr if x<0]
    for i in range(len(pos)):
        arr[2*i]=pos[i]
        arr[2*i+1]=neg[i]

def main():
    data=sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it=iter(data)
    T=int(next(it))
    out_lines=[]
    for _ in range(T):
        N=int(next(it))
        arr=[int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        rearrange(arr)
        out_lines.append(' '.join(map(str,arr)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__=='__main__':
    main()
